---
title: Serve
---

*Calepin* uses the `preview` subcommand for local development.

# Preview

`calepin preview` renders the collection or document, starts a local server, and watches for changes. When a `.qmd` file is saved, the document is re-rendered and the browser reloads automatically.

For a collection:

```
calepin preview _calepin.toml
calepin preview _calepin.toml -p 4000
calepin preview _calepin.toml -q
```

For a single file:

```
calepin preview file.qmd
calepin preview file.qmd -p 8080
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-p`, `--port` | Server port (default: 3456) |
| `-t`, `--target` | Output target |
| `-s`, `--set` | Override YAML metadata fields |
| `-q`, `--quiet` | Suppress progress messages |

# Static file server

When given a directory path, `calepin preview` acts as a plain static file server --- no rendering, no watching, no live reload. This works with any directory that contains an `index.html`, not just *Calepin* collections.

```
calepin preview _site
calepin preview docs -p 8080
calepin preview /path/to/any/website
```
